message:
fix(vdbe): prevent writable cursor fallback to MemDatabase on pager failures

SAFETY FIX: Writable cursors must never fall back to MemDatabase when
a pager transaction exists but pager reads fail. Allowing this fallback
silently routes writes to non-durable in-memory storage, creating:

1. Data loss: writes appear successful but aren't persisted to WAL
2. Divergence: concurrent readers see pager state while writer sees Mem state
3. Corruption: commit sequence numbers and MVCC snapshots become inconsistent

Root cause:
The labeled-block fallback path in open_storage_cursor checked only:
  `has_mem_table && !self.reject_mem_fallback`
This allowed WRITABLE cursors to fall back when pager reads failed,
violating the critical safety invariant documented in the function.

Fix:
- Add `&& !writable` guard to both fallback conditions:
  1. Pager read error path (line ~9997)
  2. Invalid page with MemDatabase path (line ~10196)
- Track decision_reason in variable for accurate tracing
- Update comments to clarify read-only vs writable fallback rules
- Add has_mem_table to refusal log for debugging

The only writable "bootstrap" case remains: zero-initialized root pages
that can be safely initialized in-place via the pager.

Added tests:
- test_open_storage_cursor_write_read_failure_does_not_fallback_to_mem:
  Verifies writable cursor open fails (returns false) when pager read
  errors occur, even with MemDatabase owning the table

- test_open_storage_cursor_write_invalid_page_does_not_fallback_to_mem:
  Verifies writable cursor open fails on invalid pager pages (root > db_size)
  even when MemDatabase has the table

Both tests confirm no cursor is left installed after failed opens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
